      <title>Sources Detail LeBron James' Most Likely Landing Spots For 2026-27 Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284809/Sources-Detail-LeBron-James-Most-Likely-Landing-Spots-For-2026-27-Season</link>
      <description><p>LeBron James faces a pivotal free agency decision this summer, with multiple teams emerging as realistic destinations for what could be his 24th NBA season. ESPN spoke with more than a dozen league sources to outline the leading options for the 41-year-old.</p>
<p>The Los Angeles Lakers will carry roughly $50 million in projected cap space with James' $52.6 million salary off the books. Sources told ESPN the franchise would welcome a return, though the salary James seeks will heavily influence the outcome.</p>
<p>The Cleveland Cavaliers represent what one Eastern Conference executive called the <br />"cleanest fit." Multiple league and Cavs sources told ESPN that if James wants a third stint, the franchise will find a way to make it work. A sign-and-trade involving Jarrett Allen could allow James to earn significant money while giving the Lakers a needed frontcourt piece.</p>
<p>"I always thought it made the most sense for him to go back home," a West scout said.</p>
<p>The Golden State Warriors offer a different appeal. James and Stephen Curry developed what coach Steve Kerr described as a "bromance" during the 2024 Paris Olympics. The Warriors also feature close James ally Draymond Green.</p>
<p>The Denver Nuggets drew perhaps the strongest basketball endorsement from sources. Teaming James with three-time MVP Nikola Jokic drew this assessment from one Western Conference executive: "Who is the only guy on [James'] level from a basketball IQ standpoint in the league? Go there and team up with that guy."</p>
<p>The New York Knicks surfaced as another possibility. James has repeatedly called Madison Square Garden his favorite arena, and the Knicks' front office has deep personal ties to him. Leon Rose was once James' agent, while William Wesley has had a personal connection since James was a teenager. However, a team source acknowledged the franchise must weigh disrupting its established core.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 31 Mar 2026 16:12:30 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284809/Sources-Detail-LeBron-James-Most-Likely-Landing-Spots-For-2026-27-Season</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/James_LeBron_lal_240202.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Stephen Curry Participates In Full Scrimmage, Eyes Sunday Return</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284811/Stephen-Curry-Participates-In-Full-Scrimmage-Eyes-Sunday-Return</link>
      <description><p>Golden State Warriors guard Stephen Curry received medical clearance to participate in a five-on-five scrimmage Tuesday, marking the most significant milestone in his recovery from a persistent right knee injury. A potential return Sunday against the Houston Rockets is now the target if he continues to progress.</p>
<p>Curry spoke candidly about his situation after the workout.</p>
<p>"It felt great," Curry told ESPN. "I'm checking the boxes. But with this, it's always unpredictable because I don't know how the knee will respond because it hasn't responded well in the past attempts to get to this five-on-five level. I'm right where I need to be for right now and I'm hoping that it continues until the weekend."</p>
<p>Steve Kerr offered a measured response to the development.</p>
<p>"Gotta see how [his body] responds to the scrimmage and we'll take it from there," Kerr said.</p>
<p>Curry will miss his 26th and 27th straight games Wednesday against the San Antonio Spurs and Thursday against the Rockets before Sunday's earliest possible return date.</p>
<p>The injury originated after a workout in Minneapolis on January 24. Curry briefly played through discomfort before exiting Golden State's home loss to the Detroit Pistons on January 30 and has not appeared since. An expected return following the All-Star break was delayed after his knee reacted poorly to on-court work in Los Angeles.</p>
<p>Lead medical decision maker Rick Celebrini held Curry out of contact work for weeks, clearing him only Tuesday morning for live action.</p>
<p>Golden State sits 36-39, currently holding the 10th seed in the Western Conference after losing 16 of their last 25 games. The franchise hopes to get Curry sufficient playing time before the play-in tournament, where two consecutive victories would secure the eighth playoff seed.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 31 Mar 2026 23:06:15 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284811/Stephen-Curry-Participates-In-Full-Scrimmage-Eyes-Sunday-Return</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Curry_Stephen_gsw_220523.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Lucas Williamson, Girzzlies Sign 10-Day Contract</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284810/Lucas-Williamson-Girzzlies-Sign-10-Day-Contract</link>
      <description><p>Wing Lucas Williamson has signed a 10-day contract with the Memphis Grizzlies.</p>
<p>Williamson (6-4, 205) has appeared in 46 games (15 starts) for the Memphis Hustle and Windy City Bulls during the 2025-26 NBA G League Tip-Off Tournament and regular season has averaged 7.2 points, 2.3 rebounds, 1.3 assists and 1.1 steals in 23.7 minutes. The 27-year-old has competed in 139 NBA G League games (46 starts) over four seasons with the Ontario Clippers, Hustle and Bulls. Williamson has a career PER of 7.9 in the G League.</p>
<p>Williamson played five years at Loyola Chicago, where he was named the Missouri Valley Conference Defensive Player of the Year twice (2021, 2022) and was a member of the Ramblers' 2018 NCAA Final Four team. He went unselected in the 2022 NBA Draft.</p>
<p>Williamson is represented by <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/info/agent_clients/Keith-Kreiter/153">Keith Kreiter</a> and <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/info/agent_clients/Sam-Cipriano/782">Sam Cipriano</a>.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Jaden Ivey To Receive Full Salary Despite Being Waived By Bulls For Conduct Issues</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284812/Jaden-Ivey-To-Receive-Full-Salary-Despite-Being-Waived-By-Bulls-For-Conduct-Issues</link>
      <description><p>Jaden Ivey will collect his entire $10.1 million salary this season despite the Chicago Bulls releasing the guard for conduct detrimental to the team, sources briefed on the matter confirmed. Ivey, 24, will become an unrestricted free agent once he clears waivers.</p>
<p>In the days before his release, Ivey made a series of comments during Instagram livestreams, calling Catholicism "a false religion" and stating that God would not hear prayers from sinners. On Monday, he criticized Pride Month celebrations, including those organized by the NBA, characterizing them as representing "unrighteousness."</p>
<p>Bulls head coach Billy Donovan addressed the decision publicly.</p>
<p>"I think there's a certain level of standards and expectations that are here," Donovan said. "We have people from all different walks of life working in the building and players from all different walks of life. We've got to all be professional. I think there's got to be a high level of respect for one another, and we got to help each other and then be accountable to those standards."</p>
<p>Ivey was finishing the final year of a four-year rookie contract, having been selected fifth overall by the Detroit Pistons in the 2022 NBA Draft. Chicago acquired him in February via a three-team trade deadline deal that sent Kevin Huerter and Dario Saric to Detroit and Mike Conley to the Bulls.</p>
<p>Ivey appeared in just four games for Chicago and had not played since February 11. The team shut him down last week due to a sore left knee.</p>
<p>Despite the public explanation for his release, Ivey was waived through the standard waiver process and may sign with any NBA franchise once he clears.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 31 Mar 2026 23:10:13 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284812/Jaden-Ivey-To-Receive-Full-Salary-Despite-Being-Waived-By-Bulls-For-Conduct-Issues</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Ivey_Jaden_det_221114.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>J.B. Bickerstaff After Pistons Clinch First Division Title In 18 Years: 'We Expect More'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284815/JB-Bickerstaff-After-Pistons-Clinch-First-Division-Title-In-18-Years-We-Expect-More</link>
      <description><p>The Detroit Pistons clinched their first division title since the 2007-08 season following a 127-116 win over the Toronto Raptors on Tuesday night.</p>
<p>"You take pride in understanding how hard it is to do these things in this league," coach J.B. Bickerstaff said after the win. "You take pride in the fact that you have a group of young men who have grown and achieved certain milestones.</p>
<p>"It's a part of the process, and we expect more."</p>
<p>Jalen Duren finished the game with 31 points on 12-13 shooting. </p>
<p>Daniss Jenkins, who had 21 points and 5 assists, echoed Bickerstaff's sentiment.</p>
<p>"We knew we were trying to do something special this year," he said. "It's great to accomplish something like this, but we have our eyes on the playoffs and doing some damage there. We'll celebrate this tonight and get back to work tomorrow."</p>
<p>Two years after going 14-48, the Pistons are 55-21 on the season. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 01 Apr 2026 02:35:20 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284815/JB-Bickerstaff-After-Pistons-Clinch-First-Division-Title-In-18-Years-We-Expect-More</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Bickerstaff_JB_det_25027.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Jalen Brunson, Jayson Tatum Among 12 Finalists For NBA's Twyman-Stokes Teammate Of The Year</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284808/Jalen-Brunson-Jayson-Tatum-Among-12-Finalists-For-NBAs-Twyman-Stokes-Teammate-Of-The-Year</link>
      <description><p>The NBA revealed the 12 finalists for the 2025-26 Twyman-Stokes Teammate of the Year Award on Tuesday. Current players across the league will vote to determine the winner from the nominated group.</p>
<p>The award recognizes the player best exemplifying selfless play, on and off-court leadership, mentorship and overall dedication to his team.</p>
<p>Six finalists were selected from each conference. The Eastern Conference nominees are Desmond Bane of the Orlando Magic, Jalen Brunson of New York Knicks, Pat Connaughton of Charlotte Hornets, Jrue Holiday of Portland Trail Blazers, Duncan Robinson of Detroit Pistons, Marcus Smart of Los Angeles Lakers, Jayson Tatum of Boston Celtics and Garrett Temple of Toronto Raptors.</p>
<p>The Western Conference finalists are De'Aaron Fox of San Antonio Spurs, Jeff Green of Houston Rockets, DeAndre Jordan of New Orleans Pelicans and Jaylin Williams of Oklahoma City Thunder.</p>
<p>Stephen Curry of the Golden State Warriors claimed the award in 2024-25.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 31 Mar 2026 15:55:56 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284808/Jalen-Brunson-Jayson-Tatum-Among-12-Finalists-For-NBAs-Twyman-Stokes-Teammate-Of-The-Year</guid>

      <title>Candace Parker, Elena Delle Donne, Amar'e Stoudemire Headline 2026 HOF</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284814/Candace-Parker-Elena-Delle-Donne-Amare-Stoudemire-Headline-2026-HOF</link>
      <description><p>Candace Parker, Elena Delle Donne, Amar'e Stoudemire, and Doc Rivers will be inducted into the Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame for the Class of 2026, according to Shams Charania of ESPN.</p>
<p>Parker, who won WNBA titles with three different teams, is the only player to win Rookie of the Year and MVP in the same season.</p>
<p>Delle Donne is also a two-time league MVP and won a title in 2019 with the Mystics.</p>
<p>Rivers is eighth all-time in coaching wins and won a title with the Celtics in 2008.</p>
<p>Stoudemire was a six-time All-Star and the 2003 Rookie of the Year.</p>
<p>The formal ceremonies for the Hall of Fame are scheduled in August. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 01 Apr 2026 02:29:18 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284814/Candace-Parker-Elena-Delle-Donne-Amare-Stoudemire-Headline-2026-HOF</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_realgm.jpg" />
   </item>

You can witness this Western Conference clash streaming live on Peacock.</p>

<p>With playoff seeding on the line, the atmosphere in Inglewood will be electric. This Tuesday night showdown is essential viewing for NBA fans, and Peacock is your home for the complete broadcast.</p>

      <title>Masai Ujiri Wants To Return To NBA To Win Another Championship</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284800/Masai-Ujiri-Wants-To-Return-To-NBA-To-Win-Another-Championship</link>
      <description><p>Former NBA front office executive Masai Ujiri may be out of the league, but that doesn't mean he's been away from the game. Ujiri has joined the ownership group for the Toronto Tempo ahead of their inaugural season in the WNBA. And the former Nuggets and Raptors front office leader has his eyes on a return to the NBA too.</p>
<p>"I've been learning more about our game, the NBA, looking at trends and what we might be missing by studying other leagues," Ujiri said. "I spend a lot of my time trying to do that because one of my main goals is to win another championship. I want to win with the Tempo, and I want to win another NBA championship because I wasn't able to celebrate and be happy because of that police incident that happened to me."</p>
<p>When the Raptors won the 2019 NBA Finals, Ujiri was barred from joining his team in the initial on-court celebration by a security guard at Oracle Arena. That incident led to each party filing lawsuits which were ultimately dropped. But the experience is still part of what's driving the former Raptors lead decision maker.</p>
<p>"There's a hunger in me because of what happened," Ujiri said. "I want to win again so I can actually enjoy it."</p>
<p>While away from the NBA for the first time in years, Ujiri said he's kept busy by chauffeuring his kids around to their various activities He's also been active in helping to grow sports facilities and infrastructure throughout Africa.</p>
<p>"We just completed the sports city [in Rwanda] and now I'm doing things with the U.N. in the Sahel [region of north-central Africa]. And we're going to open in Guinea, Sierra Leone and Mauritania in a few weeks. We made a pledge to build the sports complexes, and they're absolutely incredible," Ujiri said.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 31 Mar 2026 09:39:50 EDT</pubDate>

      <title>Evan Mobley Continuing Post-All-Star Surge After Being Challenged By Kenny Atkinson</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284798/Evan-Mobley-Continuing-Post-All-Star-Surge-After-Being-Challenged-By-Kenny-Atkinson</link>
      <description><p>Cleveland Cavaliers big man Evan Mobley recorded a season-best 34 points and 17 rebounds Monday against the Utah Jazz, the latest statement performance in a remarkable second-half turnaround fueled by an All-Star break conversation with head coach Kenny Atkinson.</p>
<p>Mobley shot 15 of 21 from the field, adding three blocks in the 122-113 victory. All shot attempts came inside the paint, including nine dunks and four layups.</p>
<p>Atkinson said he pushed Mobley to use the All-Star snub as motivation.</p>
<p>"I told him to go home during All-Star break and work," Atkinson said. "Like, 'You've got to come out of All-Star break with a chip on your shoulder.' Injuries. Whatever. Not a great start. Didn't make the All-Star team. You should come out of All-Star with a chip on your shoulder to prove people wrong a little bit. That's what he has done."</p>
<p>Mobley described the exchange as straightforward and purposeful.</p>
<p>"It was a simple conversation about using it as a chip on my shoulder and knowing the value that I bring to this team," Mobley said. "This past month or so, I have been doing a good job of that and I'm going to continue to keep growing and take that into the playoffs."</p>
<p>Across 18 post-All-Star contests, Mobley is averaging 19.7 points on 62.2% shooting, 9.6 rebounds and 1.5 blocks. Cleveland outscores opponents by 6.9 points per 100 possessions with him on the floor.</p>
<p>Atkinson credited newly acquired guard James Harden, who ranks second in assists on Mobley baskets since joining Cleveland seven weeks ago.</p>
<p>"He's gotta be one of the best bigs in the league since the All-Star break," Atkinson said.</p></description>

      <description><p>Boston Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla wants no part of Coach of the Year conversations, delivering a characteristically blunt assessment of the award Monday despite being considered one of the leading candidates.</p>
<p>"I don't need it," Mazzulla said. "I think it's a stupid award. They shouldn't have it. And it's more about the players. It's more about the work that the staff puts in. It's just that simple. I really don't ever want to be asked or talk about it again. It's just that dumb. So, the players play. It's about them. Staff works their ass off. I'm grateful to have them."</p>
<p>Mazzulla's candidacy is well founded regardless of his indifference. He has guided Boston to its fifth consecutive 50-win season through a significant offseason roster overhaul and Jayson Tatum missing most of the year with a torn Achilles. The Celtics currently sit second in the Eastern Conference.</p>
<p>Atlanta Hawks coach Quin Snyder offered an expansive endorsement of Mazzulla's work.</p>
<p>"Anytime your roster changes, when you lose a player of Jayson Tatum's caliber, you adjust," Snyder said. "What Joe's done is figure out a way to play that maximizes the personality that they have. And I think ultimately that's fundamentally, when I look at them and watch them play, why I would say that he's done such a terrific job."</p></description>

      <title>Jaden Ivey Waived By Bulls After Anti-LGTBQ Comments</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284792/Jaden-Ivey-Waived-By-Bulls-After-Anti-LGTBQ-Comments</link>
      <description><p>Jaden Ivey will be waived by the Chicago Bulls following his recent anti-LGBTQ comments on social media. </p>
<p>The Bulls acquired Ivey from the Detroit Pistons ahead of the trade deadline and was set to be a restricted free agent in the offseason. Ivey was recently shut down for the season by the Bulls with a knee injury. </p>
<p>"The world proclaims LGBTQ, right? They proclaim Pride Month and the NBA does too. They show it to the world. They say, 'Come join us for Pride Month to celebrate unrighteousness,'" said Ivey. </p>
<p>Ivey went on Instagram Live, where he continued to talk about religion and the NBA.</p>
<p>"They're liars, bro. This is lying," Ivey said. "They're lying saying my conduct is detrimental to the team. That's a lie. Ask any one of them coaches in there, 'Was I a good teammate?' All I'm preaching about is Jesus Christ and they waived me. They say I'm crazy, right? I'm psycho."</p>
<p>He later said, "I didn't get myself waived. I was in the gym today. I was rehabbing, doing what was required of my job."</p>
<p>Billy Donovan responded to the decision to part ways with Ivey on Monday.</p>
<p>"I think there's a certain level of standards and expectations that are here," Donovan said. "I mean, we have people from all different walks of life working in the building and players from all different walks of life, right? So, the first thing is, everybody comes with their own personal experiences. But one is, we've got to all be professional. I think there's got to be a high level of respect for one another, and we got to help each other and then be accountable to those standards."</p></description>

      <title>Tom Dundon Group's $4.25 Billion Purchase Of Blazers Unanimously Approved By Board Of Governors</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284791/Tom-Dundon-Groups-$425-Billion-Purchase-Of-Blazers-Unanimously-Approved-By-Board-Of-Governors</link>
      <description><p>The NBA Board of Governors has unanimously approved the sale of the Portland Trail Blazers to a group led by Dallas businessman Tom Dundon, a league source told The Athletic. The deal is structured at an approximate total value of $4.25 billion.</p>
<p>The transaction ends the Allen family's ownership era dating to 1988. It is structured in two stages: an 80.1 percent stake closes March 31 at a $4 billion valuation, with the remaining 19.9 percent closing no later than September 1, 2028, at a $4.5 billion valuation. During the interim period, former Blazers chairman Bert Kolde will observe board meetings without governance authority.</p>
<p>Dundon's purchasing group includes Portland's Sheel Tyle of investment firm Collective Global, Chicago's Marc Zahr of Blue Owl, the Cherng family of Las Vegas, founders of Panda Express, and Freedom Mortgage CEO Stanley Middleman, who also holds a partial stake in the Philadelphia Phillies.</p>
<p>Dundon currently serves as majority owner of the NHL's Carolina Hurricanes, which he acquired in 2018 for $420 million. He recently sold 12.5 percent of that stake at a $2.66 billion valuation while retaining majority control. Carolina has reached the playoffs in eight straight seasons under his stewardship.</p>
<p>The Blazers are seeking $600 million to renovate the Moda Center. Oregon's Legislature has already committed $365 million, funded through income tax revenues from team-related activity. Additional contributions from Multnomah County and the city of Portland remain pending council votes.</p>
<p>Paul Allen purchased the franchise in 1988 for $70 million. Following his death in 2018, his sister Jody assumed governance. The Allen estate listed the team for sale in May 2025, with all proceeds designated for charitable distribution.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 30 Mar 2026 16:09:08 EDT</pubDate>

      <title>Bobby Portis Says Giannis Antetokounmpo Will Not Play Again This Season For Bucks</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284789/Bobby-Portis-Says-Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Will-Not-Play-Again-This-Season-For-Bucks</link>
      <description><p>Milwaukee Bucks forward Bobby Portis publicly stated that teammate Giannis Antetokounmpo will not return to the court this season, citing both the team's eliminated playoff status and broader business considerations surrounding a potential trade.</p>
<p>Portis, entering his sixth season with Milwaukee, made the remarks during an appearance on FanDuel TV's "Run It Back." The Bucks were mathematically eliminated from postseason contention Saturday, sitting at 29-45 with eight games remaining.</p>
<p>"I don't think he'll play another game this year, for sure," Portis said. "Just being smart as a pro, right now, I don't think it's smart for him to play."</p>
<p>Antetokounmpo has suited up for just 36 games this season amid a string of injuries. He has missed the club's last seven contests following a left knee hyperextension. The Athletic previously reported he resisted the organization's suggestion to shut him down entirely.</p>
<p>A dispute over his status has intensified in recent days. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284657/NBPA-Critical-Of-Bucks-Injury-Management-Of-Giannis-Antetokounmpo">The National Basketball Players Association released a statement claiming Antetokounmpo is healthy and the Bucks are preventing his return</a>. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284688/Doc-Rivers-Pushes-Back-On-NBPA-Tanking-Claims-Says-Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Simply-Not-Healthy">Doc Rivers directly contested that assertion</a>, maintaining the star has not received medical clearance.</p>
<p>Portis did not shy away from the trade speculation surrounding the two-time MVP.</p>
<p>"If y'all talking about trading him, you don't want any injury to knock value off or whatever," Portis said. "That's just business-wise, and then player-wise, obviously, you gotta look at just the total picture of what's going on. We're 10 games behind for the 10th seed and (with) nine games left, we can't even make up ground."</p>
<p>Bucks general manager Jon Horst fielded trade inquiries ahead of the February deadline before opting to retain Antetokounmpo. Renewed discussions are anticipated this offseason. </p></description>
